comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: If all the factors were equal, the tournament system would be great, there’s 3-4 factors that can drastically affect performance of a flock. Just to name a few The USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) recently claimed that delaying the Poultry Grower Payment Rule for 18 months would save $5.2 million in administrative costs. Let’s call that what it is: ...

Good evening, I am reaching out both as a contract grower, healthcare professional, and proud American. I own a 12 house broiler farm in Northeast Texas and grow for JBS/Pilgrim Pride. I love life as a grower! The sense of pride I have raising a product that feeds America is indescribable! It’s the same satisfaction I feel when I treat a sick patient back to...

comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: When AMS finalized the Payment Systems rule, AMS explained there was uncertainty as to whether the benefits would outweigh the costs.[5] ---Comment;The current tournament pay system is expensive to run each grower is tracked in terms of bird placement, feed consumption, hen flock age, livability, % condemn, live weight, flock density, M/F ratio, avg age, chi...

I’ve been a grower in broiler industry for 11 years in that time I’ve seen the tournament as it is today only favor those who either give favors to managers of the company I grow for so that this particular ones stay on top regardless of what their true ranking in the tournament system is while honest growers suffer losses and in some cases selling out cause...

The most concerning part of this entire situation is the actual poultry grower (contract farmer) who grows the chicken, not producer (integrator) seems to have little to no input into the matter. The financial risk that the grower assumes when building or buying and operating a facility is extremely high to not have any input. To start, there is no fairness ...

I bought a poultry farm 3 years ago 6 boilers. With hopes and dreams of my family being able to run and live on a farm for generations to come. This way they could watch their kids grow and grandchildren grow. Showing that hard work and many sacrifices that was and still is being made so people can have food on there tables. I’ve been seeing where the proposed grower payment has been postponed. I’m a broiler grower of almost 9 years, and through the tournament system I have ranked really well, and I have ranked really bad, all doing the same farm management flock to flock. I have seen swings of $15,000 to the bad all the while it costing me the same money to raise the bad flock ...

There needs to be something done if the tournament system stays. There is so much variance in the quality of baby chicks coming out of all the hatcheries. This makes the tournament completely unfair from the start. I will attach a picture of our current mortality if I can to show what I mean. Our flock came on 3 truck loads, 243,000 birds. One load was compl...

You should be ashamed to put this off for another 18 months! Us poultry farmers are going bankrupt to grow birds for these company’s and they could care less. Our utilities are so high we have to take out loans and they are putting the family farms out of business. Trump flat out lied to us when he said he would cut our utilities by 50% the first year and he...

I am a supporter of the tournament system but capital improvements she be founded 50% by the integrator and 50% by the farmer. If the integrator has to invest in the capital improvements then it’s truly an improvement for the welfare of the flock. If they have no skin in the game then they can require anything at anytime for no reason.

An overwhelming number of contract growers want this rule in place. There are too many factors that go into the tournament system that make it outdated and unfair to growers. Factors like bird and feed quality as well as timing of weather with bird placement. These factors are out of growers hands. American farm bureau has spoken to usda in face to face conv...

As a poultry farmer with decades of hands-on experience, I can tell you plainly—this delay is not just disappointing, it is deeply harmful. Growers like myself have been navigating years of rising costs, inflation, and increasing operational expenses with little relief. Labor , utilities, maintenance, and supplies have all surged, yet our compensation struct...
Oh, come on, you've had plenty of time to study this and it's time to just step up and do what's right. Don't be a pawn of the most corrupt administration the USA has ever struggled under.

The Trump administration better wake up!! We the farmers made up a huge part of the reason why He got in office in the first place. This rule that was passed 2 years ago was the only thing the poultry farmers has had done in their favor in 20 plus years, but it looks like poultry company lobbyists has written somebody a fat check to screw the American farmer...

We currently love the new rule, no longer worrying about losing a contract over feed conversion. No longer getting punished for the bad birds the company provides via a contract when we pay 40 cents for each chick but get 25 week pullets and as a result hundreds die and our bottom line suffers and they are weaker then normal chicks. We have been contract poultry farmers for many years, and we were hopeful to see an end to the current tournament system. Too often, this system creates a situation where farmers are effectively competing against the company rather than being supported by it. Farmers are required to raise whatever birds are delivered to their farms, regardless of quality. Fo...

The past changes to the grower contract which required release of Breeder details (breeder age) has shed light on the iniquities in the tournament pay system. While the industry least cost their efforts; growers are not given that choice (see attached). In my opinion after 20 years as a contract grower, the integrator needs to be held responsible for action ...

We grow the company’s chickens , with the company’s programs in our computers , I grow better weight birds and my livability is around 93% usually which is above average, but I am penalized thousands of dollars every batch because of feed conversion, when I only use very little more feed , and no one from the company can tell me what will help with that. I don't understand the delay "companies are all ready there Wayne-Sanderson and others"

I'm a poultry grower in South Central Pennsylvania. We have a fourhouse farm for an integrator, that has organic chickens in Pennsylvania. With organic chickens, chick quality and feed quality is a big deal. This proposed delay of the Poultry Grower Payment Systems and Capital Improvement Systems rule is yet another kick in the teeth to contract poultry growers. The tournament pay system has been unfair and exploitative for years — pitting farmers against each other, slashing pay based on arbitrary rankings, and forcing growers to sink massive money into capital...

All we want is fair pay for the work we do .Funny how the government can control how much water your washing machine can use but they let the big corporations squeeze the growers till they can't hardly make it , guess everyone can't have lobbyist.

Very disappointed in USDA being unprepared to truly address the issues . Poultry growers are chained to the integrators willingness to give raises, compensation for quality issues, either in bird quality or feed quality, differences between these are something farmers like myself have no control over but is directly how we are paid. I disagree with delaying this rule and urge implementation as soon as possible. Farmers need guide rails and clarity on what is expected without tournament style pricing. Help the farmer, help the food network in America.
The postponement is ridiculous we need something done now us poultry farmers are hurting
This is not helping the framers!! This is bull crap! USDA getting it wrong again.

AS a contract poultry grower, I'm very disappointed in this decision. Just another example of being bought off by big Ag "JBS".
They have to increase the payment this in not right specially the winter time we are not making money at all to pay gas that it so please help us to pay more

Good Morning I would really like you to implement it this year and not delay any further please. Some flocks I even can’t pay my mortgage. So kindly implement it this year and do not delay any further.
Please allow the rule to be implemented as scheduled. do not delay this..we growers need protection and to be treated fairly. thankyou
